p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Yellow pot; dense foliage cluster; turgid, succulent leaves.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Stable throughout the 5-day sequence; no significant morphological shifts observed.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_inference: Optimal. The leaves maintain high turgor pressure and consistent coloration, indicating successful moisture regulation.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Black pot; two primary leaves with smaller secondary growth; central placement.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: Leaf margins remain stable; no further necrosis progression observed since the T-5 baseline.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_inference: Recovering. The lack of further margin degradation suggests the previous stressor has been mitigated.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Black pot; two leaves; white rabbit scale anchor present.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: The apical leaf shows a static, slight tip necrosis; no expansion of the lesion observed.
p3_pothos.visual_health_inference: Stable. The lesion is contained, indicating the plant has reached a homeostasis point despite VPD stress.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Black pot (shared with p2); small sprout near the rim.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: Growth remains minimal but consistent; no signs of chlorosis or wilting.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_inference: Stable. The seedling is maintaining its current developmental stage.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Consistent moisture levels across all pots; no signs of surface crusting or hydrophobic patches.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: White particulate matter (perlite/top-dressing) remains evenly distributed; no fungal activity or desk debris.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: The sequence shows a period of stabilization. The transition from T-5 to CURRENT indicates that the environmental interventions have successfully halted the progression of the previously noted stress markers.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of 'Resilient Stasis'. All plants are showing signs of adaptation to the current indoor micro-climate.
vision_report.anomalies: None. The white material on the soil surface is confirmed as a successful outcome of user-applied top-dressing/care.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit confirms that the garden has moved from a state of active stress (as seen in the T-5 image) to a stable, maintained state. The visual evidence of the leaf margins on p2 and the tip necrosis on p3 confirms that the degradation has ceased, validating the current care regimen. The plants are effectively managing the VPD environment.
